Literature Digest

Jun 17 – Jul 01, 2026  ·  Chuong Lab, CU Boulder
981 scanned
11 preprints
20/20 journals
keyword + AI

AI Summary

The standout paper this fortnight is from the Burns and Insco labs (Tanu et al., Cell Reports): recurrent truncating mutations in ZC3H18 — a nuclear RNA surveillance factor lost in 30% of cancers — stabilize oncogenic ERV RNA and drive melanoma in zebrafish, with human melanoma cell growth dependent on ZC3H18-regulated ERV RNA. This is a direct mechanistic parallel to the lab's LTR10 and SP140/ERV work in colorectal and ovarian cancer, and raises whether ZC3H18 loss contributes to ERV reactivation phenotypes in PEO1 or HCT116 models. Complementing this, Cedric Feschotte's new Nature Reviews Genetics review (Feschotte, 2026) frames TEs as broad catalysts of evolutionary innovation — a timely high-profile synthesis of the conceptual backbone of the lab's entire research program.

The rest of the digest is dominated by cGAS-STING biology across six papers: lactate directly inhibiting STING to enable tumor immune evasion (Dong et al., Immunity), nuclear cGAS driving STING-independent Wnt/β-catenin metastasis in TNBC via PKCα phosphorylation (Zhang et al., Molecular Cell), VEGFR2-AKT1 as a negative STING regulator with therapeutic synergy potential (Han et al., Cell Reports), and mtRNA release from hyperfused mitochondria activating RIG-I/MAVS (Yasuda et al., Cell Reports). On the splicing/isoform front: U2AF1 mutations rescuing KRAS-induced exon skipping in lung cancer (Nature Genetics), nitric oxide globally regulating alternative splicing via PTBP1 S-nitrosylation (Molecular Cell), and a NUMB isoform switch driving tumor-specific signaling changes in breast cancer — all resonant with the lab's TE exonization and isoform biology work.

Curated Highlights 24 papers · 7 topics

TE Biology 2
Recurrent ZC3H18 mutations stabilize oncogenic endogenous retroviral RNA.
Jun 24, 2026 · Tanu Tanzina, Insco Megan L et al. · Cell Reports
ZC3H18 truncating mutations stabilize and relocalize ERV RNA to the cytoplasm, driving melanoma onset and growth. → article
Transposable elements as catalysts of evolutionary innovation.
Jul 01, 2026 · Feschotte Cedric · Nature Reviews Genetics
Review synthesizing transposable elements as catalysts of regulatory, structural, and functional evolutionary innovation. → article
Cancer 5
PKCα-mediated nuclear translocation of cGAS stabilizes β-catenin and drives metastasis.
Jun 18, 2026 · Zhang Qimin, Zhang Peijing et al. · Molecular Cell
PKCα phosphorylates cGAS at Ser120, enabling nuclear translocation and STING-independent Wnt/β-catenin activation driving TNBC metastasis. → article
VEGFR2 inhibition potentiates STING-mediated antitumor immunity.
Jun 18, 2026 · Han Fangping, Zhang Conggang et al. · Cell Reports
VEGFR2-AKT1 suppresses STING signaling; Ki8751 VEGFR2 inhibition synergizes with cGAMP to elicit antitumor immunity in vivo. → article
Lactate binds and inhibits the innate immune sensor STING to promote tumor immune evasion
Jun 30, 2026 · Dong Guo, Ying Meng, Qingqing Yang, Shudi Luo, Xiaoming Jiang, Yanhua Zheng, Lihui Wu, Suyao Li, Hongfei Jiang, Shan Li, Zheng Wang, Min Li, Qingang Wu, Liwei Xiao, Rongxuan Zhu, Xuxiao He, Yanyan Hu, Hong Zhao, Xianglai Ye, Yingying Tong, Jing Fang, Daqian Xu, Jun Xu, Ying Yuan, Zhimin Lu · Immunity
Tumor lactate, produced via PKM2-LDHA, directly binds and inhibits STING to suppress innate immune surveillance. → article
Immune cell–intrinsic STING activation drives tumor ferroptosis via AA-mediated suppression of ACSL4 lactylation in colorectal cancer
Jun 23, 2026 · Lina DingWenqi DuJing ZhuYuxiang ZhangXingyue WangLinfeng LiBuhui LiuXiaohong WangQingling WangDongsheng PeiaDepartment of Pathology, School of Basic Medical Sciences, Xuzhou Medical University, Xuzhou 221004, ChinabDepartment of Human Anatomy, School of Basic Medical Sciences, Xuzhou Medical University, Xuzhou 221004, Chinachttps://ror.org/048q23a93Department of Gastroenterology, Xuzhou Clinical School of Xuzhou Medical University, Xuzhou Central Hospital, Xuzhou 221009, China · PNAS

The mutational landscape of STING-induced immunity.
Jun 24, 2026 · Zhang Bing, Ablasser Andrea et al. · Nature
Deep mutagenesis maps STING gain-of-function variants producing constitutive interferon or NF-κB signaling with differential potency. → article

RNA Biology 5
U2AF1 mutations rescue deleterious exon skipping induced by KRAS mutations
Jul 01, 2026 · David M. Walter, Matthew Meyerson et al. · Nature Genetics
KRAS G12S creates U2AF1-binding sites causing KRAS exon skipping; co-occurring U2AF1 mutations rescue skipping to restore oncogenic KRAS in lung cancer. → article

Two steric zipper clusters in sTDP-43's unique C-terminal tail drive cytoplasmic aggregation and mislocalization independently of the canonical prion-like domain. → article
